phases.name: Year 1
phases.subtitle: Explore
phases.description: Introduction to agricultural education and teacher preparation foundations.

milestones.label: Explore agricultural education careers
milestones.description: History, philosophy, and career opportunities in agricultural education. Students observe high school ag programs and develop their teaching identity.

milestones.label: Begin teacher education pathway
milestones.description: Foundations of teaching including classroom management, educational psychology, and NC teaching standards. Students complete early field observations in public schools.

milestones.label: Develop academic writing skills
milestones.description: Academic writing, critical thinking, and communication skills for teacher preparation and professional practice.

milestones.label: Build agricultural science foundations
milestones.description: Biological science foundations that support agricultural content knowledge for secondary teaching.

phases.name: Year 2
phases.subtitle: Engage
phases.description: Culturally responsive pedagogy, special education, and youth program management build teaching competencies.

milestones.label: Study culturally responsive pedagogy
milestones.description: Multicultural education, culturally responsive teaching practices, and equity-focused curriculum design. Students develop lesson plans that honor diverse cultural perspectives in agricultural education.

milestones.label: Learn exceptional children education
milestones.description: Introduction to special education law, IEP development, and differentiated instruction for students with diverse learning needs in agricultural education settings.

milestones.label: Master youth program management
milestones.description: FFA chapter management, supervised agricultural experience (SAE) programs, and youth leadership development. Students design and implement agricultural education extracurricular activities.

milestones.label: Build animal science content knowledge
milestones.description: Animal science content knowledge for secondary agricultural education teaching. Covers species identification, production systems, and veterinary science basics.

phases.name: Year 3
phases.subtitle: Develop
phases.description: Methods courses, educational technology, and clinical practice build teaching proficiency.

milestones.label: Master agricultural education foundations
milestones.description: Advanced pedagogical methods for teaching agricultural subjects including laboratory instruction, project-based learning, and competency-based assessment.

milestones.label: Integrate educational technology
milestones.courseRef: AGED 302 — Educational Technology in Ag Ed
milestones.description: Technology integration in agricultural education including precision agriculture tools, virtual labs, and multimedia instruction. Students design technology-enhanced lesson plans.

milestones.label: Study adult education methods
milestones.description: Adult learning theory, extension education methods, and community-based agricultural education program development.

milestones.label: Complete pre-clinical teaching experience
milestones.description: Extended field experience in secondary agricultural education classrooms. Students teach lessons, manage FFA activities, and receive mentor teacher feedback.

phases.name: Year 4
phases.subtitle: Launch
phases.description: Full-semester student teaching and licensure preparation complete the pathway to NC Teaching License.

milestones.label: Begin senior clinical practice
milestones.description: Advanced clinical practice in secondary agricultural education. Students assume increasing responsibility for classroom instruction, FFA advising, and SAE supervision.

milestones.label: Master program planning & evaluation
milestones.description: Agricultural education program design, curriculum mapping, advisory board management, and comprehensive program assessment aligned with NC agricultural education standards.

milestones.label: Study environmental education methods
milestones.description: Environmental literacy, outdoor education, and place-based learning in agricultural education contexts. Connects agricultural education to sustainability and natural resource conservation.

milestones.label: Complete full-semester student teaching
milestones.description: Full-semester clinical practice in a secondary agricultural education program. Students assume full teaching responsibilities, manage FFA chapters, supervise SAEs, and demonstrate all NC teaching competencies. Culminating experience for licensure eligibility.
